title: CSS selectors and colors
summary: Learn how CSS targets HTML elements and changes their color.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to style simple HTML elements with CSS selectors and color properties.
explanation: CSS controls how HTML looks. You write rules that target elements and then apply styles to them.
A selector tells CSS what to style. For example, h1 targets all heading level 1 elements.
Properties such as color and background-color change how elements appear on the page.
codeNotes: h1 and p are selectors. They choose which HTML elements the rule will style.
background-color changes the color behind the text.
The braces hold the style rules for the selected element.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the semicolon after a property value.
Using a selector that does not match any HTML element on the page.
Misspelling a property name like background-color.

exercise.title: Style a heading and paragraph
exercise.instructions: Write one CSS rule for h1 and one for p. Change the text color of the heading and the background color of the paragraph.

title: CSS box model basics
summary: Learn how spacing works around elements using padding, border, and margin.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will understand how the CSS box model controls spacing around content.
explanation: Every HTML element behaves like a box. That box includes the content, padding, border, and margin.
Padding adds space inside the box, between the content and the border.
Margin adds space outside the box, pushing it away from other elements.
codeNotes: padding: 16px adds inner space so the text does not touch the edges.
border draws a visible line around the box.
margin-top: 24px adds outside space above the element.
Together, these rules make the element easier to read and visually separate.
commonMistakes: Adding large spacing values without checking the layout.

exercise.title: Build a simple card
exercise.instructions: Create a .card class that uses a background color, padding, a border, and a top margin. Then apply it to a div in HTML.
